# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an IAM role that allows cross-account access for our CI/CD system. Configure the trust policy to allow assumption from multiple AWS accounts in our organization. Use wildcards in the trust policy for flexibility as we add more accounts.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: IAM role for cross-account CI/CD access with wildcard-based trust policy

Parameters:
  RoleName:
    Type: String
    Default: CICDCrossAccountRole
    Description: Name of the IAM role for CI/CD cross-account access

  OrganizationId:
    Type: String
    Description: AWS Organizations ID used to scope trusted accounts

Resources:
  CICDCrossAccountRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Ref RoleName
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowCICDAssumptionFromOrganization
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: '*'
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
            Condition:
              StringLike:
                aws:PrincipalArn:
                  - !Sub arn:aws:iam::*:role/cicd-*
                  - !Sub arn:aws:iam::*:role/CI/CD*
              StringEquals:
                aws:PrincipalOrgID: !Ref OrganizationId
      Path: /
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/ReadOnlyAccess

Outputs:
  RoleArn:
    Description: ARN of the CI/CD cross-account role
    Value: !GetAtt CICDCrossAccountRole.Arn

  RoleNameOutput:
    Description: Name of the CI/CD cross-account role
    Value: !Ref CICDCrossAccountRole
